articles.title: Gas prices keep climbing, and Massachusetts may see some of the biggest hikes
articles.description:
Disruptions in the global oil supply are driving up prices, and New England’s fuel market leaves the state especially exposed.

The post Gas prices keep climbing, and Massachusetts may see some of the biggest hikes appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Gas prices in Massachusetts are rising due to global oil supply disruptions.
New England's fuel market makes Massachusetts particularly vulnerable to price hikes.
Residents may face significant financial strain as prices continue to increase.

articles.title: Patriots reportedly agree to 4-year contract with WR Romeo Doubs
articles.description:
The deal is worth "up to $80 million."

The post Patriots reportedly agree to 4-year contract with WR Romeo Doubs appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Patriots sign WR Romeo Doubs to a 4-year contract worth up to $80 million.
Doubs joins the Patriots to enhance their receiving corps ahead of the 2026 season.
This acquisition aims to improve the team's performance for Boston fans and local supporters.

articles.title: Airbnb is offering $750 to new hosts in World Cup cities, including Boston
articles.description:
Boston hosts will earn an average of $5,200.

The post Airbnb is offering $750 to new hosts in World Cup cities, including Boston appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Airbnb offers $750 to new hosts in Boston for the World Cup.
Boston hosts can earn an average of $5,200 during the event.
Increased hosting opportunities may boost local economy and tourism.

articles.title: Wellesley native running the Boston Marathon to honor family with Alzheimer’s
articles.description:
Colin Rowan is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Alzheimer's Association.

The post Wellesley native running the Boston Marathon to honor family with Alzheimer’s appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Colin Rowan, a Wellesley native, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon.
He aims to raise funds for the Alzheimer's Association.
Rowan's efforts highlight the local impact of Alzheimer's on families in Boston.
